question_html: If {tex}\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)=\log \sqrt{x^{2}+y^{2}} {/tex}, prove that {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x+y}{x-y}{/tex}.
explanation: Given that, {tex} \log \sqrt{x^2+y^2}=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex} Prove that, {tex} \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x+y}{x-y} {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \log \left(x^2+y^2\right)=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right) \left[\frac{d}{d x}(\log x)=\frac{1}{x}\right] {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{1}{x^2+y^2}\left(2 x+2 y \frac{d y}{d x}\right)\right)=\frac{1}{1+\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)^2}\left(\frac{x \frac{d y}{d x}-y}{x^2}\right) {/tex} {tex} \frac{\not 2\left(x+y \frac{d y}{d x}\right)}{2\left(x^2+y^2\right)}=\frac{x^2}{x^2+y^2}\left(\frac{x \frac{d y}{d x}-y}{x^2}\right) {/tex} {tex} \frac{d y}{d x}(y-x)={-y-x}{/tex} {tex} \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x+y}{x-y} {/tex} Hence proved
solution: Given that, {tex} \log \sqrt{x^2+y^2}=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex} Prove that, {tex} \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x+y}{x-y} {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \log \left(x^2+y^2\right)=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right) \left[\frac{d}{d x}(\log x)=\frac{1}{x}\right] {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{1}{x^2+y^2}\left(2 x+2 y \frac{d y}{d x}\right)\right)=\frac{1}{1+\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)^2}\left(\frac{x \frac{d y}{d x}-y}{x^2}\right) {/tex} {tex} \frac{\not 2\left(x+y \frac{d y}{d x}\right)}{2\left(x^2+y^2\right)}=\frac{x^2}{x^2+y^2}\left(\frac{x \frac{d y}{d x}-y}{x^2}\right) {/tex} {tex} \frac{d y}{d x}(y-x)={-y-x}{/tex} {tex} \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x+y}{x-y} {/tex} Hence proved
solution_html:
Given that,
{tex} \log \sqrt{x^2+y^2}=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex}
Prove that,
{tex} \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x+y}{x-y} {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \log \left(x^2+y^2\right)=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right) \left[\frac{d}{d x}(\log x)=\frac{1}{x}\right] {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{1}{x^2+y^2}\left(2 x+2 y \frac{d y}{d x}\right)\right)=\frac{1}{1+\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)^2}\left(\frac{x \frac{d y}{d x}-y}{x^2}\right) {/tex}
{tex} \frac{\not 2\left(x+y \frac{d y}{d x}\right)}{2\left(x^2+y^2\right)}=\frac{x^2}{x^2+y^2}\left(\frac{x \frac{d y}{d x}-y}{x^2}\right) {/tex}
{tex} \frac{d y}{d x}(y-x)={-y-x}{/tex}
{tex} \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x+y}{x-y} {/tex}
Hence proved

explanation: We know that, principal value branch of {tex}\cos ^ { - 1 }{/tex} x is [0, 180°]. explanation: The given inverse trignometric function is {tex}\cos ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { - 1 } { 2 } \right) + 2 \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { 1 } { 2 } \right){/tex} Now, {tex}\cos ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { - 1 } { 2 } \right) + 2 \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { 1 } { 2 } \right){/tex} {tex}= \left[ \pi - \cos ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { 1 } { 2 } \right) \right] + 2 \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { 1 } { 2 } \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ \because \cos ^ { - 1 } ( - x ) = \pi - \cos ^ { - 1 } x ; \forall x \in [ - 1,1 ] \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \pi - \cos ^ { - 1 } \left( \cos \frac { \pi } { 3 } \right) \right] + 2 \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \sin \frac { \pi } { 6 } \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ \because \cos \frac { \pi } { 3 } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \text { and } \sin \frac { \pi } { 6 } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \pi - \frac { \pi } { 3 } \right] + 2 \times \frac { \pi } { 6 }{/tex} {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c } { \because \cos ^ { - 1 } ( \cos \theta ) = \theta ; \forall \theta \in [ 0 , \pi ] } \\ { \text { and } \sin ^ { - 1 } ( \sin \theta ) = \theta ; \forall \theta \in \left[ \frac { - \pi } { 2 } , \frac { \pi } { 2 } \right] } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 2 \pi } { 3 } + \frac { \pi } { 3 } = \frac { 2 \pi + \pi } { 3 } = \pi{/tex}
solution: The given inverse trignometric function is {tex}\cos ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { - 1 } { 2 } \right) + 2 \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { 1 } { 2 } \right){/tex} Now, {tex}\cos ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { - 1 } { 2 } \right) + 2 \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { 1 } { 2 } \right){/tex} {tex}= \left[ \pi - \cos ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { 1 } { 2 } \right) \right] + 2 \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { 1 } { 2 } \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ \because \cos ^ { - 1 } ( - x ) = \pi - \cos ^ { - 1 } x ; \forall x \in [ - 1,1 ] \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \pi - \cos ^ { - 1 } \left( \cos \frac { \pi } { 3 } \right) \right] + 2 \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \sin \frac { \pi } { 6 } \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ \because \cos \frac { \pi } { 3 } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \text { and } \sin \frac { \pi } { 6 } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \pi - \frac { \pi } { 3 } \right] + 2 \times \frac { \pi } { 6 }{/tex} {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c } { \because \cos ^ { - 1 } ( \cos \theta ) = \theta ; \forall \theta \in [ 0 , \pi ] } \\ { \text { and } \sin ^ { - 1 } ( \sin \theta ) = \theta ; \forall \theta \in \left[ \frac { - \pi } { 2 } , \frac { \pi } { 2 } \right] } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 2 \pi } { 3 } + \frac { \pi } { 3 } = \frac { 2 \pi + \pi } { 3 } = \pi{/tex}
solution_html:
The given inverse trignometric function is {tex}\cos ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { - 1 } { 2 } \right) + 2 \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { 1 } { 2 } \right){/tex}

Now,{tex}\cos ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { - 1 } { 2 } \right) + 2 \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { 1 } { 2 } \right){/tex}
{tex}= \left[ \pi - \cos ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { 1 } { 2 } \right) \right] + 2 \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { 1 } { 2 } \right){/tex}
{tex}\left[ \because \cos ^ { - 1 } ( - x ) = \pi - \cos ^ { - 1 } x ; \forall x \in [ - 1,1 ] \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \left[ \pi - \cos ^ { - 1 } \left( \cos \frac { \pi } { 3 } \right) \right] + 2 \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \sin \frac { \pi } { 6 } \right){/tex}
{tex}\left[ \because \cos \frac { \pi } { 3 } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \text { and } \sin \frac { \pi } { 6 } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \left[ \pi - \frac { \pi } { 3 } \right] + 2 \times \frac { \pi } { 6 }{/tex}
{tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c } { \because \cos ^ { - 1 } ( \cos \theta ) = \theta ; \forall \theta \in [ 0 , \pi ] } \\ { \text { and } \sin ^ { - 1 } ( \sin \theta ) = \theta ; \forall \theta \in \left[ \frac { - \pi } { 2 } , \frac { \pi } { 2 } \right] } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 2 \pi } { 3 } + \frac { \pi } { 3 } = \frac { 2 \pi + \pi } { 3 } = \pi{/tex}
